title: Heatwave alert: Temperatures to rise across Dakshina Kannada, Udupi till April 23 - Mangalore Today
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Karnataka
full_text:
Mangaluru, Apr 21, 2026: A rise in temperature is likely across the district from April 21 to 23, according to a forecast.
The intensity of heat is expected to increase in Mangaluru, Ullal, Bantwal, Moodbidri, Puttur, and Kadaba regions. Similarly, temperatures are also likely to rise in Brahmavar, Hebri, and Karkala taluks of Udupi district.
The Karnataka State Natural Disaster Monitoring Centre (KSNDMC) has issued this alert, noting that temperatures in several parts of the district have already been exceeding 37°C in recent days.
With the possibility of intense heatwave conditions in these areas, authorities have advised the public to take necessary precautions.
